questions.question: Unit of $K$ for third order reaction is .............
A reaction is of the first order relative to $A$ and is of second order relative to $B$. What will be the effect on rate if the concentrations of $A$ and $B$ are doubled?
The order of a reaction for an esterification process is ....
Which equation is true to calculate the energy of activation, if the rate of reaction is doubled by increasing temperature from $T_1 \text{ K}$ to $T_2 \text{ K}$.
When plotted a graph of concentration $\rightarrow$ Time for zero order reaction, then the value of Slope is -
For Fourth Order Reaction, what is the unit of $K$ ?
What is the formula to find the value of $t_{1/2}$ for a Zero Order Reaction ?
For a First order reaction, the initial concentration of a reactant is $0.05\text{ M}$. After $45\text{ minutes}$ it is decreased by $0.015\text{ M}$. Calculate half reaction time. $(t_{1/2})$
Give relation between half reaction time $(t_{1/2})$ and initial concentration of reactant for $(n - 1)$ order reaction.
The half life period for a first order reaction is ________.

options.D: Inversely proportional to the square of the concentration

questions.question: According to Arrhenius equation, the slope of $\log k \rightarrow \frac{1}{\text{T}}$ plot is ________.
The value of rate constant for a first order reaction is $2.303 \times 10^{-2} \text{ sec}^{-1}$. What will be the time required to reduce the concentration to $1/10$ th of its initial concentration?
Total order of reaction $X + Y \rightarrow XY$ is 3. The order of reaction with respect to X is 2. State the differential rate equation for the reaction.
$X \xrightarrow{\text{Step-I}} Y \xrightarrow{\text{Step-II (slow)}} Z$ is a complex reaction. Total order of reaction is 2 and Step - II is slow step. What is molecularity of Step-II?

Reaction $3\text{ClO}^- \rightarrow \text{ClO}_3^- + 2\text{Cl}^-$ occurs in following two steps. (i) $\text{ClO}^- + \text{ClO}^- \xrightarrow{K_1} \text{ClO}_2^- + \text{Cl}^-$ (Slow step) (ii) $\text{ClO}_2^- + \text{ClO}^- \xrightarrow{K_2} \text{ClO}_3^- + \text{Cl}^-$ (Fast step) then the rate of given reaction = ________.
At 298 K temperature the activation energy for the reaction $x_2 + y_2 \rightarrow 2xy + 20$ kJ is 15 kJ. What will be the activation energy for the reaction $2xy \rightarrow x_2 + y_2$?
The half-life period for a radioactive substance is 15 minutes. How many grams of this radioactive substance is decayed from 50 gram of substance after one hour?
The rate constant value for a reaction is $1.75 \times 10^2 \text{ L}^2 \text{ mol}^{-2} \text{ sec}^{-1}$. The half-life period $t_{1/2} \propto$ ____ .
The units for the rate constant and the rate of reaction are same for a reaction. What will be the order of the reaction?
At $27^\circ\text{C}$ temperature, time required for $75\%$ completion of a first order reaction is $20\text{ seconds}$. What will be its rate constant?
Which statement is incorrect for a catalyst?

options.A: It decreases the activation energy of a reaction
options.B: It increases the proportion of products in less time
options.C: It does not affect the equilibrium constant
options.D: It increases the free energy change for the reaction

questions.question: Instantaneous rate of reaction for the reaction $3A + 2B \rightarrow 5C$ is ______
In a reaction $A \rightarrow B$, if the concentration of reactant is increased by 9 times then rate of reaction increases 3 times. What is the order of reaction?
Which statement is incorrect for collision theory?

options.A: The reactant experiencing fruitful collisions are converted to products
options.B: There must be certain minimum energy for the reactant experiencing collision
options.C: The collision of the reactant molecules should be from any direction
options.D: The collision between in the reacting molecules is essential

questions.question: In the presence of a catalyst, the heat evolved or absorbed during the reaction .......
Which of the following graph has intercept equal to zero?

Time required to decompose $\text{SO}_2\text{Cl}_2$ to half of its initial amount is $40\text{ minutes}$. If the decomposition is a first order reaction, what will be the rate constant of the reaction?
For a reaction, $K = 4.5 \times 10^{-4}\text{ L mol}^{-1}\text{ s}^{-1}$. What is order of reaction?
For first order reaction, the value of slope for graph of $\log \frac{[R]_0}{[R]} \rightarrow t$ is __________.
The rate constant for a first order reaction is $60\text{ s}^{-1}$. How much second will it take to reduce the initial concentration of the reactant to its $1/16$th value?
What is the value of slope when graph plotted of $\log \frac{[R]_0}{[R]}$ Vs $t\text{ (time)}$ for first order reaction?
A reaction is first order with respect to a reactant A and second order with respect to reactant B. What is the effect of rate when concentration of both A and B increased by doubled?
For which of the following graph of first order reaction the value of slope will be $K/2.303$?
Which will be the unit of rate constant for the reaction having $\text{Rate} = K[\text{A}]^{1/2}.[\text{B}]^{3/2}$?
A reaction is first order in terms of A and second order in terms of B. What will be the rate of reaction, if concentration of B is increased two times?
For any reaction the rate constant $	ext{K} = 2.3 	imes 10^{-5} 	ext{ mol}^{-3/2} 	ext{ L}^{3/2} 	ext{S}^{-1}$; then the order of reaction will be ______.
Which of the following statements is incorrect for a reaction carried out in presence of catalyst?

options.A: Potential energy of reactants and products change
options.B: Equilibrium constant of the reaction does not change
options.C: There is no change in Gibbs energy of the reaction
options.D: The activation energy of the reaction decreases

questions.question: Which of the following graphs is correct for a first order reaction $	ext{R} ightarrow 	ext{P}$?
Reaction $2	ext{A} ightarrow 	ext{B} + 3	ext{C}$ is zero order reaction. If $	ext{K} = 3.5 	imes 10^{-4} 	ext{ mol L}^{-1} 	ext{ S}^{-1}$; What will be the rate of production of 'C'?
Half life period for certain zero order reaction is $10\text{ min}$. then how much time is required for this reaction to complete $100\%$?

The half-life for radioactive decay of $^{14}\text{C}$ is $5730\text{ years}$. An archaeological artifact containing wood had only $80\%$ of the $^{14}\text{C}$ found in a living tree. Which is the correct formula for age $(t)$ of the sample?
According to Arrhenius equation which of the following statement is correct?

options.A: Decrease in temperature or increase in Activation Energy will increase rate of reaction.
options.B: Increase in temperature and increase in Activation Energy will increase rate of reaction.
options.C: Increase in temperature or decrease in Activation Energy will increase rate of reaction.
options.D: Decrease in temperature and decrease in Activation Energy will increase rate of reaction.

questions.question: Select correct reaction for the given rate. $\text{rate} = -6 \frac{d[A]}{dt} = -4 \frac{d[B]}{dt} = 3 \frac{d[C]}{dt} = 4 \frac{d[D]}{dt}$
